summary: Machine policy for the Widgets feature. Good for a quieter desktop with fewer Microsoft content surfaces, but it removes the Widgets board and its taskbar entry point.

setting.name: Widgets Feature
setting.casual_explanation: Windows Widgets can show a board with news, weather, and other feed content. This policy decides whether the Widgets feature is allowed on the device at all.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable Widgets to reduce feed-style content, remove the taskbar entry point, and keep the desktop quieter.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning Widgets off removes an extra content surface and taskbar entry point, but it also removes the board for people who actually use it.
setting.professional_notes: The NewsAndInterests Policy CSP uses the allow-form name AllowNewsAndInterests, and NewsAndInterests.admx maps the machine Group Policy to HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Dsh\AllowNewsAndInterests with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: The device is not forcing a Widgets policy and Windows keeps its normal default behavior.

allowed_values.label: Widgets allowed
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is enabled in allow-form and the Widgets feature is available on the device.

allowed_values.label: Widgets blocked
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is disabled in allow-form and the Widgets board and its taskbar entry point are not available.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes AllowNewsAndInterests = 0, which matches the documented blocked state.

windows_defaults.label: Windows machine-policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows 10 and Windows 11 client systems that support Widgets policy

states.rationale: The device policy is not configured by default.

windows_defaults.notes: Microsoft documents the allow-form default as 1, and the local ADML help text says Widgets are turned on by default unless changed in settings.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: People who use the Widgets board
avoid_for: Users who want a quieter desktop

states.rationale: Leave Widgets under normal Windows control.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want the normal Windows Widgets behavior.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want to remove feed-style content and its taskbar entry point.
tradeoffs: The device does not enforce a Widgets restriction.

recommended_profiles.label: No widgets
intended_for: Users who want fewer feed surfaces

states.rationale: Block the Widgets feature through policy.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want a quieter desktop and no Widgets board.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want access to the Widgets board and its content.
tradeoffs: The Widgets board and taskbar entry point are removed.
Users lose the built-in Widgets experience.

evidence.title: Microsoft Policy CSP: NewsAndInterests / AllowWidgets
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents the allow-form Widgets policy, its default value, and the related registry path and value name.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft NewsAndInterests.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file maps the machine policy to HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Dsh\AllowNewsAndInterests with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft NewsAndInterests.adml help text
evidence.summary: The local ADML help text says Widgets are on by default unless changed in settings, and enabling the block removes the board and taskbar entry point.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes AllowNewsAndInterests = 0 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Existing tweak provenance record
evidence.summary: The repo already tracks provenance for this tweak in the current catalog output.

decision.why: Local Microsoft Widgets policy files document the exact allow-form policy surface, numeric mapping, and default behavior. The app writes the documented blocked value.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: <policy name="AllowNewsAndInterests" class="Machine" ... key="SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Dsh" valueName="AllowNewsAndInterests"> ... <enabledValue><decimal value="1" /></enabledValue> ... <disabledValue><decimal value="0" /></disabledValue>; NewsAndInterests.adml: This policy specifies whether the widgets feature is allowed on the device. Widgets will be turned on by default unless you change this in your settings.
validation_proof.notes: Local official ADMX defines the exact machine-policy key, value name, and 1/0 mapping; local ADML confirms this is the Widgets allow-form policy and documents its default behavior.
